short_def: A themed collection of recorded sessions on AI Now Guide, grouping all the work on one topic, site, or workflow into a single place.
definition:
A channel is the top-level grouping for related work on AI Now Guide. Each channel covers one ongoing thread, for example a site rebuild, a research project, or a recurring workflow, and contains the sessions recorded for that thread in order.

Channels give the video library a stable structure. Instead of a single undifferentiated feed, visitors can pick a channel that matches what they're interested in (AI search, digital asset portfolios, website rebuilds, tools and workflows, and more) and follow it from the first session to the latest.
example: The "Build Log" channel collects general progress sessions across the AI Now Guide project itself, while a channel like "AI Search Lab" focuses specifically on AI search and SEO experiments. A viewer who only cares about AI search can subscribe to that channel without wading through unrelated sessions.
nuance: A channel is a container, not a tutorial series. Sessions inside a channel are recorded as the work happens, so the channel reflects the actual order of decisions, including dead ends, rather than a curated step-by-step course.

seo.title: Channel | AI Now Guide Glossary
seo.description: What a channel is on AI Now Guide: a themed collection of build-in-public sessions covering one topic, site, or workflow.
